theoremName: Hardy multiplier reduction for signed Volterra transport
compressedOperator: T = C K E on the Green-minimizer plus-profile range

compressedIdentityStatus.label: compressed multiplier identity
compressedIdentityStatus.reason: The integrated minus profiles equal C K applied to the lifted plus profiles.

greenMinimalLiftStatus.label: Green-minimizer minimal lift
greenMinimalLiftStatus.reason: This is the remaining analytic theorem: the Euler-Lagrange Green lift must be shown to be the minimal contraction lift for the compressed Hardy multiplier.
greenMinimalLiftStatus.blocker: Use the trace-fiber Euler-Lagrange equation to show the boundary concomitant from integrating C K E by parts vanishes on the completed trace image.

correctedConclusion: The explicit pointwise Hardy multiplier is identified and exactly reproduces the integrated minus features from the lifted plus features.  The remaining step is not algebraic identification of T; it is proving that the Green-minimizer right inverse E makes the compression C K E contractive, with boundary terms killed by the Euler-Lagrange trace equations.
nextProofTarget: Write the integration-by-parts formula for C K E in the lifted (s,u) variables and express its boundary concomitant as the Euler-Lagrange trace-fiber residual Q(f_x,h), which is zero for all h in ker R.
